free_text: Alpha induced reactions on 114Cd and 116Cd: An
experimental study of excitation functions
Enriched 114Cd and 116Cd electroplated onto Cu (12.5
microns) backing.
isotopic composition:
114Cd                 116Cd
Two stacks containing, respectively, 8 enriched 116Cd
and 10 enriched 114Cd foils were irradiated in the
external 38.5 MeV alpha beam at the VUB cyclotron.
Irradiations were made for about 1 h at a constant,
low beam current (about 90 nA). The copper backings
served as a monitor foils as well as recoil nuclei
catchers. The samples were measured about 2 h after
EOB and assessment of the activity continued for a
couple of times over a 9 months decay period, at
different sample detector distances. Independent and
cumulative cross sections were determined.
The SRIM 2006 code was used for estimation of the
energy degradation in each foil of the stacks.
Correction of 25% in beam intensity and 0.5 MeV in
incident particle energy were necessary to introduce
based on monitor reactions.
Chapter 4
A reliable detection efficiency curve is difficult to
be determined for a relatively narrow energy domain
13-60 keV, therefore, efficiencies measured for higher
energies photons of 152Eu were considered. A fit with
a rational function was than performed over a wide
energy range 13-1400 keV giving rise to a mean
deviation of 12% in respect with the measured values
for the 17-60 keV X-ray energy.
The incident energy was estimated at 38.9 MeV
with an uncertainty of 0.1 MeV. Due to the variations
in foils thickness and energy spread, the uncertainty
in the median energy estimation is increasing along the
stack, reaching at a maximum of 4.4 MeV in the last
foil.
The total uncertainty is evaluated at 8-13%.
The following individual uncertainties are taken into
account:                                          D4204
the number of target nuclei including
non-uniformity (5%),
detector efficiency (5%)
incident particle intensity (5%) and
absolute abundance of the used gamma
determination of the peak areas including
statistical errors (1-5%),
R.A.Rebeles, 2008.
